description: This figure presents two carbon concentration maps obtained from atom probe tomography (APT) analysis, demonstrating spinodal decomposition behavior in Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C lightweight austenitic steels aged at 530 °C for 10 h. The left map corresponds to the steel with 0.006 wt.% phosphorus, while the right map corresponds to the steel with 0.043 wt.% phosphorus. Both maps use a color scale ranging from blue (2 at.% carbon) to red (6 at.% carbon), with x and y axes spanning from -30 nm to 30 nm. The high-phosphorus steel exhibits significantly larger, more prominent carbon-enriched regions compared to the low-phosphorus steel where carbon concentration fluctuations are smaller and less distinct. These results confirm that higher phosphorus content greatly accelerates the spinodal decomposition rate during aging, supporting the core conclusion that phosphorus promotes κ-carbide precipitation kinetics, which leads to reduced impact toughness and dynamic fracture toughness in age-hardened high manganese aluminum steels.
